title: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Garfield Heights, OH
metaTitle: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Garfield Heights, OH
metaDescription: Section 8 apartments for rent in Garfield Heights, OH: how CMHA vouchers work, what protections don't apply here, and nearby voucher-friendly homes.
primaryKeyword: section 8 apartments for rent Garfield Heights ohio
secondaryKeywords: voucher friendly rentals near slavic village
answerBox: Garfield Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb directly south of Cleveland's Slavic Village and Union-Miles neighborhoods. Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, and Garfield Heights isn't among the local suburbs with their own protection ordinance. CMHA administers Section 8 countywide; our nearest voucher-friendly rental homes sit just north in Slavic Village and Buckeye-Shaker.

sections.h2: Are there Section 8 apartments for rent in Garfield Heights?
body: Some landlords there do accept Housing Choice Vouchers, but acceptance comes down to each landlord's own policy rather than a legal requirement. Garfield Heights is a Cuyahoga County suburb directly south of Cleveland's Slavic Village and Union-Miles neighborhoods, and — unlike a handful of other county suburbs — it is not reported to have its own ordinance barring landlords from refusing an applicant for using a voucher.

Rent Finder Cleveland does not currently work with units inside Garfield Heights, but our closest managed homes sit just across the city line in **Slavic Village** (ZIP 44105), our single largest concentration of Section 8-ready rental homes, and **Buckeye-Shaker** (ZIP 44120) a bit farther east. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) to see current availability in those neighborhoods, or tell our team if a Garfield Heights address specifically matters to your search.

sections.h2: How Section 8 works in Garfield Heights and the rest of Cuyahoga County
body: Whether or not you end up renting inside Garfield Heights, your Housing Choice Voucher comes from the same place: [CMHA](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php), the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority. CMHA's preliminary application stays open online year-round with no closing date, and applicants are selected from that list through periodic random mini-lotteries rather than strict first-come-first-served order — a name might be reached within days, or it may take months, per CMHA's own guidance. Applying requires an active email address, Social Security numbers and birth dates for the whole household, and household income details.

Once a household is issued a voucher, the specific unit it wants to rent still has to pass a housing-quality inspection before assistance is paid. HUD is moving from the older HQS standard to NSPIRE, with a final transition deadline of February 1, 2027 for the [Housing Choice Voucher program](https://www.hud.gov/helping-americans/housing-choice-vouchers). Our [step-by-step CMHA guide](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/how-to-apply-section-8-cleveland-cmha/) covers the application in more detail.

sections.h2: What Section 8 protections don't apply in Garfield Heights
body: Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, so a landlord in most of Cuyahoga County can lawfully decline an applicant simply for using a voucher — the exceptions are a short list of individual suburbs that have passed their own local ordinances. Reporting names Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, and Warrensville Heights as examples; Garfield Heights and the City of Cleveland itself are not on that list. That means whether a Garfield Heights landlord accepts a voucher comes down to that landlord's own policy, not a legal requirement.

Read our [source-of-income guide](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/source-of-income-discrimination-cleveland-ohio/) for the fuller jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction breakdown, and always confirm a specific listing's voucher policy directly before applying.

Since we don't yet manage homes inside Garfield Heights itself, here's what similar homes rent for across our current Cleveland selection, concentrated in the neighboring Slavic Village and Buckeye-Shaker areas just to the north. Two-bedroom homes are our most common size and average around $961 a month; three-bedroom homes average around $1,250, with both figures reflecting a mix of duplexes, single-family houses, and small multi-family buildings.

body: Garfield Heights sits along Cleveland's southeast border, and [RTA's bus network](https://www.riderta.com/routes) connects it to Tower City and the rest of the rail system, since the suburb has no rail station of its own. The nearest neighborhoods where we work with homes, Slavic Village and Buckeye-Shaker, are both served by RTA bus routes and sit closer to the Blue and Green light-rail corridor that runs through neighboring Shaker Heights.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with Section 8 apartments in Garfield Heights?
faq.a: Not currently. Our closest voucher-friendly rental homes sit just north in Slavic Village (44105) and Buckeye-Shaker (44120), both accepting Housing Choice Vouchers and kept HUD-inspection-ready. Tell our leasing team if a Garfield Heights address matters and we'll point you to the nearest fit.

faq.q: Is Garfield Heights required to accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: No. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, and Garfield Heights is not reported among the small group of Cuyahoga County suburbs — such as Cleveland Heights and Warrensville Heights — that have passed their own voucher-protection ordinances. Acceptance there depends on each landlord's own policy.

faq.q: Who administers Section 8 vouchers for Garfield Heights?
faq.a: CMHA, the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority, administers Housing Choice Vouchers countywide, including Garfield Heights. Its preliminary application is open online year-round, with applicants selected through periodic random mini-lotteries rather than strict order.

faq.q: What's the closest Section 8-friendly rental to Garfield Heights?
faq.a: Our largest concentration of voucher-friendly homes is in Slavic Village (ZIP 44105), directly north of Garfield Heights, followed by Buckeye-Shaker (ZIP 44120). Both accept Housing Choice Vouchers and are HUD-inspection-ready.

faq.q: How much does a 2-bedroom rent near Garfield Heights?
faq.a: Across our current Cleveland selection near Garfield Heights, two-bedroom homes average roughly $961 a month, typically ranging from about $750 to $1,100 depending on the neighborhood and the condition of the unit.
